description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Registration for the 2026 Cultural Heritage Walks, "Enjoying Hanno's Landforms and Geology," has closed as of May 20, 2026 (Reiwa 8) because the event reached capacity.

The event was scheduled for Saturday, May 23, 9:15 a.m.–12:30 p.m., starting at Motokaji Station (Seibu Ikebukuro Line). Participants would have walked about 3 kilometers to Akebono Children's Forest Park, observing fossil footprints of Palaeoloxodon namadicus (an ancient elephant) in the Iruma River riverbed and the Hanno and Butsuji rock formations that make up the Kaji Hills. The instructor was from the Kanto Plain Western Edge Hills Research Group, providing expert commentary on landform observation. The event was for high school students and older, with a fee of ¥500 (including insurance).

For further inquiries, contact the Museum / Cultural Heritage Section, Education Department, Education Board (phone: 042-972-2301; email: museum@city.hanno.lg.jp).

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kyoikubu/hakubutsukan/bunkazaitanto/bunkazai/13272.html
summary: ℹ️ 2026 Cultural Heritage Walks cancelled (registration closed as of May 20)
